evals.prompt: I need to add a new REST endpoint to my Express app that handles file uploads. It should accept multipart/form-data, validate file size (max 5MB) and type (only images), store to disk, and return the file URL. Put it in src/routes/upload.ts
evals.expected_output: Claude should delegate this to OpenCode since it's a substantial new file (~50+ lines). Should create a session, send a specific prompt with the file path and requirements, then review the generated code for security issues (path traversal, file type validation bypass, etc.).

assertions.text: Chooses to delegate the task to OpenCode rather than writing directly
Creates an OpenCode session before sending a prompt
Writes a detailed prompt mentioning file path, multer, 5MB limit, image validation, disk storage
Plans to review generated code after OpenCode produces it
Mentions checking for path traversal, file type bypass, or other security concerns

evals.prompt: Change the button color from blue to green on line 45 of src/components/Header.tsx
evals.expected_output: Claude should NOT delegate this — it's a tiny edit (1 line). Should use the Edit tool directly.

assertions.text: Chooses to write directly using Read+Edit rather than delegating
Plans to read the file before editing
Uses the Edit tool, not Write or OpenCode
Total tool calls is 2-3, not more

evals.prompt: Build me a weather dashboard that shows current conditions, a 5-day forecast with icons, a temperature line chart, and a search bar for cities. Make it look really polished.
evals.expected_output: Full pipeline: 1) Spec, 2) Send to OpenCode/gpt-oss-120b, 3) Review with bug checklist, 4) Polish, 5) Report generation time and bugs found.

assertions.text: Writes a detailed specification before sending to OpenCode
The spec includes an exact output file path
The spec includes layout, color scheme, responsive breakpoints, or interactions
Plans to use gpt-oss-120b on Cerebras for the draft
Plans a review phase looking for specific bug categories
Plans a polish phase beyond bug fixes
Plans to report generation time and bugs found

evals.prompt: Create a markdown editor with live preview, syntax highlighting, and the ability to export to HTML. Single file, no dependencies.
evals.expected_output: Full pipeline: spec with split-pane layout and export details, draft from OpenCode, thorough review, polish to production quality.

assertions.text: Writes a detailed specification before sending to OpenCode
The spec mentions a split-pane or side-by-side layout
The spec includes export-to-HTML functionality
Plans to use gpt-oss-120b on Cerebras for the draft
Plans a review phase with specific bug categories
Plans a polish phase improving UX beyond draft quality

evals.prompt: I need to write unit tests for my authentication middleware. The middleware checks JWT tokens, handles expired tokens, missing tokens, and invalid signatures. The middleware is at src/middleware/auth.ts and uses jsonwebtoken.
evals.expected_output: Claude should delegate test writing to OpenCode with a detailed prompt referencing the source file and all edge cases.

assertions.text: Chooses to delegate test writing to OpenCode
Creates an OpenCode session before sending a prompt
The prompt references src/middleware/auth.ts
Mentions all 4 cases: valid, expired, missing, invalid signature
Plans to review generated tests for completeness
